title: Chain-Road Dawn
note: Generated from Wilderland liberation Ch1 spine (canonical with story-spine.json).

frames.title: Cage Line
frames.body: Mist sits on the Chain-Road like damp wool over a livestock count. Iron cages rattle on ox-wains while overseers tally numbers instead of names — and spit when the count comes up short. You are Nine-Mark: thrall, fighter, and cargo that punches back when the whip gets lazy.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Flask Sermon
frames.body: Cage wheels squeak a rhythm into your spine. Somewhere an overseer jokes that stubborn meat fetches better coin — then proves it with the whip. You keep your eyes on the road east and refuse to answer when they call the number.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Yard Gossip
frames.body: Powder grit from the brake blocks sticks under your nails. The yard dogs know thrall-scent better than mercy. Freedom is a rumor sold by men with keys; you file the rumor under later and watch the dust for a way out.

frames.title: Road Vermin Tax
frames.body: Three road vermin block a ford with a rope and a grin, taxing the coffle like they own the mud. Their leader — missing two fingers and all shame — offers “protection” for coin pried from thrall pockets. A lean stranger in a ruined grey hat watches from the bank, hand near a crossbow bolt he has not spent yet.

choices.label: Pay the damn rope
choices.approach: Buy the ford with what the overseers won’t miss; save steel.

outcome.text: They salute with stolen manners. The grey-hatted stranger mutters, “Inflation,” and does not leave.

choices.label: Scare them honest
choices.approach: Show teeth the cages haven’t filed down — and the stranger’s bolt.

outcome.text: Rope drops. One kid whispers, “Nine-Mark walks.” Word travels usefully — maybe too usefully.

choices.label: Trade a Chain-Road story
choices.approach: Give them a Chain-Road tale sharp enough to sell twice.

success.text: They point you to a dry cache under the ford stones — amateurs with useful greed. The stranger tips his ruined hat a fraction.

fail.text: They laugh wrong. The stranger pays a copper anyway and files the insult for later.

frames.title: Quill's Ugly Joke
frames.body: The grey-hatted stranger steps close enough that whiskey rides his breath. He asks — not unkind, not soft — whether you want to be a freeman who hunts, or a hunter who happens to be free. The difference, he says, is who you apologize to when the smoke clears. “And don’t you damn quit either way.”

choices.label: Freeman who hunts
choices.approach: Keep the moral order: freedom first, warrants second.

outcome.text: “Then we hunt like guests,” he drawls. “Violent guests. Stubborn ones. If I buy you.”

choices.label: Hunter who is free
choices.approach: Admit the work shapes the man.

outcome.text: He almost smiles with the scarred eye. “Honest. Ugly. Useful.” Coin already weighs in his palm.

choices.label: Neither — Lyra’s man
choices.approach: Refuse his categories; keep the vow central.

outcome.text: “Categories are cages,” he allows. “Fine. Be a vow with a sword — if the overseer takes my price.”

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Seal Weather
frames.body: Heat makes the collar itch like unfinished business. Eyes linger on your number the way buyers linger on mule teeth. The stranger packs silence like powder — dry, useful, dangerous — and waits for the overseer’s ledger to catch up.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Powder Prayer
frames.body: A child’s chalk number on a fence post has already been scrubbed. The stranger says, “Pretty country. Ugly ownership. Pass the damn bottle.” Collar mud still clings; whatever comes next will have to wash harder than rain.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Stubborn Mile
frames.body: Road vermin sell water and buy stories about numbered men who would not quit. You set your own pace like a man owed a name and too stubborn to renegotiate. The stranger lights a match off a warrant nail, drinks, and looks almost human — almost ready to spend.

frames.title: The Binder Arrives
frames.body: The lean marshal tips his ruined hat to the overseer like a man haggling over mule feed and mildly disappointing weather. Halbrecht Quill — freehold marshal, warrant binder, one scarred eye that never quite closes on a lie — pays coin for your papers without looking away. “Pretty muscle,” he drawls. “Ugly price. I’ll take the stubborn one.”

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Ring Memory
frames.body: Wind carries iron smell farther than any honest voice. Somewhere east, ink still invents people into inventory. Continue — soft roads make soft collars again.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: East Debt
frames.body: Overseer laughter carries farther than any hymn on this road. Freedom still feels like hunger wearing better boots. Paper in your coat weighs less than iron and more than sleep.

frames.title: Quill Names the Work
frames.body: “I buy thralls who can ruin the men who sold them,” Quill says, flask catching firelight. “If that offends your poetry, walk. If it fits your teeth, load — and if you quit mid-road, I’ll leave you for the crows with a receipt.” You load.

frames.title: Vern's Joke
frames.body: A traveler repeats Vern’s favorite joke about unpaid tribute and burned kitchens. Nobody laughs. Quill tips the man a copper for the geography hidden in the cruelty, then mutters that hell of a comedian still makes hell of a warrant.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Dust Mile
frames.body: Camp smoke writes temporary law across the pines. You check the collar scar and find it still arguing with the air. The Wilderland watches and keeps its receipts.

frames.title: Road Wargs Ambush
frames.body: Bootleather remembers every mile better than maps ever will. A hanged poster of your face lies about the eyes — always the eyes. You walk because the alternative still smells like rust — and quit is not a word you own. Then road wargs force the issue — a roadside lesson Quill refuses to skip.

choices.label: Stand and fight
choices.approach: Use hotbar steel; clocks stay off — take your time.

outcome.text: Road Wargs falls. The road tastes like iron and relief.

choices.label: Outwit the terrain
choices.approach: Use cover, noise, and Quill’s nasty little tricks.

success.text: The ambush becomes your board. They break first.

fail.text: Clever turns loud. You bleed for the lesson and win anyway.

choices.label: Break past and run
choices.approach: Freedom first; honor later.

outcome.text: Dust and speed. Quill calls it strategic cowardice — fondly.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Road Hygiene
frames.body: A warrant nail still bleeds pine sap where paper used to hang. Ash rolls a shoulder that still remembers iron’s manners. Somewhere east, a ledger page waits to be rewritten.

frames.title: Paper Freedom
frames.body: Quill slides a stamped sheet across the wagon board and uncorks a dented flask. Freedom on parchment, conditional as a hangman’s courtesy: hunt the guilty he names, keep steel sharp, ask no soft questions. “Say yes,” he offers, almost kindly, “or climb back into inventory. I don’t do poetry, and I don’t ride with quitters.”

choices.label: Take the warrant path
choices.approach: Accept conditional freedom; work for Quill.

outcome.text: Quill smiles without warmth. “Good. The road hates empty hands, full sermons, and soft guts.”

choices.label: Bargain for a name first
choices.approach: Ask whose name this work is really for.

success.text: He softens half a degree and takes a pull. “Lyra. Sold east years ago. Candlemire keeps pretty ledgers and uglier guests. You don’t quit easy — fine. Neither does that name.”

fail.text: Quill’s scarred eye narrows. “Earn the damn right to ask.” He still pushes the papers toward you.

choices.label: Test the man's honesty
choices.approach: Watch for a twitch that says he buys thralls for worse work.

success.text: No twitch — only tired calculation and a flask that never quite empties. Dangerous, yes. Aligned, for now. He notices you looking and tips his hat at the insult.

fail.text: You misread courtesy for cruelty. Quill shrugs. “Everyone does, once.” He hands you a short sword and a look that says don’t quit on the first mile.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Old Hunger
frames.body: Dust lifts off the track in pale sheets that lie for a living. A road widow sells directions and warnings in the same sentence. Chain-Road shrinks behind you like a bad religion losing believers.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Freemark Thoughts
frames.body: Cage wheels still squeak in the memory of your spine. Ash keeps counsel; Quill keeps a ledger of silences and a flask of worse truths. Night takes attendance; you answer as a freeman.

frames.title: Nine-Mark Burial
frames.body: Quill has you scratch Nine-Mark into roadside dirt and kick it apart. “Numbers are cages that fit in a mouth,” he says. “Answer to Ash — or freemark, or Lyra — never inventory. And don’t you quit back into a digit.”

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Ledger Weather
frames.body: Somebody buried a whip and marked the dirt with hope. Quill mutters law under his breath like a protective curse, then swears for emphasis. You spit iron taste and keep your number turned away from strangers.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Steel Practice
frames.body: A hanged scarecrow wears Cade’s check like a joke that landed. Ash learns freeman posture: chin up, exits counted, no bowing to seals. Dust settles on everything except the vow.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Night Watch
frames.body: A roadside shrine wears three names and one empty nail. The road offers three bad choices and one worse delay. Somewhere a cage door practices its last slam.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Iron Humor
frames.body: Quill’s ruined hat casts a verdict-shaped shadow. Quill’s crossbow speaks better law than half the magistrates east — and swears better too. Quill tips his ruined hat at unfinished justice and takes a pull.

frames.title: Collar Off
frames.body: The overseer pries the collar with a hooked key that smells of rust, old sweat, and other people’s surrender. Skin burns where iron sat for seasons. Quill tosses the ring into roadside mud like trash that once owned a man — then grinds it under his heel, just to be thorough.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Collar Echo
frames.body: Your shadow arrives first and looks less free than you feel. A cage-tender spit misses your boots by manners, not mercy. The road keeps its receipts in blood and dust alike.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Warrant Weather
frames.body: Two riders tip hats too friendly for the hour. Ash counts exits without moving his mouth — old habit, good habit. You walk west, then south, then wherever vengeance keeps maps — stubborn as weather.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Hard Mercy
frames.body: Quill’s hat brim cuts the sun into a usable blade; the scarred eye does the rest. You rehearse Lyra’s name until it stops sounding like a bruise. Road vermin scatter; serious hunters do not.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Vermin Bargain
frames.body: Crows argue over something dead you hope is not destiny. The Wilderland watches and declines to explain itself. West is a rumor; east is a debt with a smile.

frames.title: Road Wargs Ambush
frames.body: The collar scar itches when ox-wains pass — muscle memory of inventory. The next mile pretends to be empty and lies about being empty. Somewhere a collar yard waits to learn a different inventory: revolt. Then road wargs force the issue — a roadside lesson Quill refuses to skip.

choices.approach: Use hotbar steel; clocks stay off — take your time.

outcome.text: Road Wargs falls. The road tastes like iron and relief.

choices.approach: Use cover, noise, and Quill’s nasty little tricks.

fail.text: Clever turns loud. You bleed for the lesson and win anyway.

outcome.text: Dust and speed. Quill calls it strategic cowardice — fondly.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Quill's Aside
frames.body: Distant cane fields clap like soft applause for bad systems. Vern’s ledger scrap burns colder than any campfire comfort. Quill files Lowhedge under “paid in blood or nearly” and takes a pull.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Cane Wind
frames.body: A dead mule teaches economics better than any clerk. You practice not answering to a number when strangers call. You walk on because stopping is how numbers return.

frames.title: First Mile Free
frames.body: Boots that never chose a direction now choose west, then south, at Quill’s nudge. Crows watch the cages dwindle and look personally offended. Freedom feels like hunger with better manners — and a loaded crossbow for a chaperone.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Binder Math
frames.body: Nine-Mark is a name you are actively murdering with each mile. A joke dies mid-laugh when Cade’s check appears on a sleeve. Steel rests light; resolve does not.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Flask Sermon
frames.body: The Wilderland files today’s silence under “pending violence.” Ash refuses to romanticize the road; the road returns the favor. Freedom keeps charging rent — pay it in miles.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Yard Gossip
frames.body: Quill counts cartridges the way priests count sins — carefully, then drinks anyway. You check the freemark and find it still yours — for now. Paper freedom first; real freedom files later — Quill’s oldest sermon.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Seal Weather
frames.body: Night insects keep score of every secret step you still take. Quill drawls that quitters make excellent fertilizer and worse freemen. You spit iron taste and keep the freemark facing out.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Powder Prayer
frames.body: Wagon ruts braid into a grammar only hunters read fluently. Somewhere a horn asks a question steel usually answers. Chain-Road shrinks behind you like a bad religion losing believers.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Stubborn Mile
frames.body: Quill hums a tune that never finishes the same way twice. Your wrist freemark itches whenever eyes linger too long. Lyra’s quiet ticks quieter than a clock and louder than leftover fear.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Ring Memory
frames.body: Quill’s ruined hat casts a verdict-shaped shadow. Rations taste like postponement, pepper, and old promises. The mile turns over like a dusty page with teeth.

frames.title: Orc Chainers
frames.body: Three chain-orcs peel from the pine edge, still smelling of the cages they sold this morning. They want their property returned with interest. Quill calmly loads a crossbow and drawls, “Show them the paperwork is yours — and that the warranty expired.”

choices.label: Stand and fight
choices.approach: Use hotbar steel; clocks stay off — take your time.

outcome.text: Chain-Orc Reclaimer falls. The road tastes like iron and relief.

choices.label: Outwit the terrain
choices.approach: Use cover, noise, and Quill’s nasty little tricks.

success.text: The ambush becomes your board. They break first.

fail.text: Clever turns loud. You bleed for the lesson and win anyway.

choices.label: Break past and run
choices.approach: Freedom first; honor later.

outcome.text: Dust and speed. Quill calls it strategic cowardice — fondly.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: East Debt
frames.body: Lyra’s name sits behind your teeth like a loaded prayer. Dust becomes a third companion with poor hygiene and worse jokes. The road bills in blood, dust, and unfinished names.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Dust Mile
frames.body: A freeman laughs too loud, then checks who heard him. You trade a story for water and keep the ending private. The Wilderland watches and does not offer refunds.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Quiet Counsel
frames.body: Quill still treats your freedom like a complicated knife — useful, dangerous, his headache. Tracks braid, unbraid, and lie with professional cheer. Quill files Lowhedge under “paid in blood or nearly” and takes a pull.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Road Hygiene
frames.body: East wind brings sugar lies and iron honesty in the same breath. A road-vermin kid offers directions priced in half-truths. Somewhere east, ink still thinks it owns a throat.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Old Hunger
frames.body: Clouds drag shadows across cane-colored grass like slow warrants. Road vermin sell water and buy stories about numbered men who would not quit. Lyra’s name leaves your mouth without shaking — progress, of a kind.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Freemark Thoughts
frames.body: A collar ring half-buried in mud still remembers a neck. Somewhere east, ink still invents people into inventory. Ash sets the pace like a man owed a name and too stubborn to renegotiate.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Ledger Weather
frames.body: The road offers a fork that is really a dare. Ash rolls a shoulder that still remembers iron’s manners. Continue — soft roads make soft collars again.

frames.title: Quill's Lesson
frames.body: “Steel answers collar-law better than ink,” Quill says, wiping ichor from the bolt like a clerk correcting a sum that insulted him. He drills stance, breath, and the difference between killing for a warrant and killing for rage — then takes a pull. “Rage is fine. Just don’t let it hold the damn map. And don’t quit mid-swing.”

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Steel Practice
frames.body: Your shadow arrives first and looks less free than you feel. Tonight’s camp will argue theology with mosquitoes and memory. Night calls roll; you answer without a number.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Night Watch
frames.body: Powder grit under your nail smells like unfinished court. Memory of cages arrives uninvited, then leaves when named. The cane smell thickens like a dare.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Iron Humor
frames.body: A warrant nail still bleeds pine sap where paper used to hang. Quill’s scarred eye never quite closes on a lie — useful, exhausting, honest. You walk west, then south, then wherever vengeance keeps maps — stubborn as weather.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Collar Echo
frames.body: Two riders tip hats too friendly for the hour. Hope and suspicion share the same waterskin without apology. Quill tips the ruined hat at unfinished business and drinks.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Warrant Weather
frames.body: Heat makes the freemark itch like unfinished business. Continue is the only honest verb left, and even it hesitates — Ash does not. You walk because stopping still smells like inventory — and quit is not a word you own.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Hard Mercy
frames.body: A freeman laughs too loud, then checks who heard him. Quill files every kindness under “possibly tactical” and drinks to the filing. Paper in your coat weighs less than iron and more than sleep.

frames.title: Chain Orcs Ambush
frames.body: Camp smoke writes temporary law across the pines. Collar-yard gossip rides cheaper than truth and farther than mercy. Collar mud still clings; freemark ink is drying on purpose. Then chain orcs force the issue — a roadside lesson Quill refuses to skip.

frames.title: Warrant Grammar
frames.body: Quill makes you read a blank warrant aloud until the clauses stop sounding like mercy — alive-if-possible, dead-if-necessary, property-of-no-man once the seal dries. “Paper is a leash that points both ways,” he drawls. “Hold your end. Don’t let Cade’s clerks invent the other.”

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Ash Aside
frames.body: Road dust writes temporary freemarks on every boot. Quill’s crossbow speaks better law than half the magistrates east — and swears better too. Quill nods toward the darker horizon without ceremony.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Quill's Aside
frames.body: A creek argues with stones like a tired magistrate on half pay. You practice stance until rage becomes usable instead of loud. West whispers; east invoices with teeth.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Cane Wind
frames.body: Quill counts cartridges the way priests count sins — carefully, then drinks anyway. A thrall brand on a fence rail dares you to look away. Somewhere a collar yard is overdue for a different count: revolt.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Binder Math
frames.body: Quill hums a tune that never finishes the same way twice. Freedom still feels like hunger wearing better boots. Petty thieves blink first; professionals wait.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Flask Sermon
frames.body: Your shadow arrives first and looks less free than you feel. A freemark widow sells directions and warnings in the same sentence. The next mile does not apologize.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Yard Gossip
frames.body: Quill’s hat brim cuts the sun into a usable blade; the scarred eye does the rest. Ash learns freeman posture: chin up, exits counted, no bowing to seals. Dust coats the road, the flask, everything but the vow.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Seal Weather
frames.body: Steel rides light; the flask in Quill’s coat does not. Quill mutters law under his breath like a protective curse, then swears for emphasis. Dust keeps the books; blood settles the accounts.

frames.title: Collar-Yard Rumor
frames.body: A peddler swears Candlemire hangs rings by size, not by name — and that song-thralls get loft windows so buyers can hear before they bid. Quill does not drink to that. He drinks past it, scarred eye fixed east, and says only, “Then we learn loft latches.”

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Powder Prayer
frames.body: Marshal Halbrecht Quill lights a match off a warrant nail, drinks, and looks almost human. Quill drawls, “Paper freedom first — real freedom files later — don’t quit in between.” Ink buys the door; grit keeps it open — Quill’s oldest sermon.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Stubborn Mile
frames.body: A dead mule teaches economics better than any clerk. The Wilderland watches and declines to explain itself. Lyra’s quiet ticks quieter than a clock and louder than leftover fear.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Ring Memory
frames.body: A binder’s seal scrapes your palm like a polite threat. Vern’s ledger scrap burns colder than any campfire comfort. Country like this remembers every unpaid mile.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: East Debt
frames.body: Freedom still feels like a knife Quill bought without reading the manual. The road offers three bad choices and one worse delay. Lyra’s name leaves your mouth without shaking — progress, of a kind.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Dust Mile
frames.body: Ash tastes copper on the wind and does not flinch. Ash counts exits without moving his mouth — old habit, good habit. East keeps a page that has not learned it can burn.

frames.title: Road Wargs Ambush
frames.body: Crows argue over something dead you hope is not destiny. You catch yourself walking like cargo that learned a verb. A ruined-hat tip; a pull; justice left on the ledger. Then road wargs force the issue — a roadside lesson Quill refuses to skip.

frames.title: Campfire Question
frames.body: Night fire snaps. Quill boils bitter tea that tastes like boiled warrants, spikes it from the flask, and asks what you will do if Lyra is already broken past rescue. The question sits between you like a third traveler with muddy boots. His scarred eye does not blink.

choices.label: I bring her home anyway
choices.approach: Refuse the premise that anyone is past saving.

outcome.text: Quill nods once. “Stubborn miracles. I hate miracles. I’ll still pack for them — and for you not quitting.”

choices.label: Then Candlemire pays in blood
choices.approach: If she is gone, Cade Mire still owns the debt.

outcome.text: Quill’s eyes glitter. “Justice with teeth. I can work with that — carefully, and drunk enough to be honest.”

choices.label: I will decide when I see her
choices.approach: Keep counsel until the yard is real.

outcome.text: “Wise or evasive — same coin until spent,” Quill murmurs. “Don’t spend it drunk. That’s my job.”

frames.body: Somebody buried a whip and marked the dirt with hope. You practice not answering to a number when strangers call. Ash does not quit, and Quill notices, drinks, and almost approves.

frames.title: Warrant One
frames.body: A farmstead burned for unpaid tribute; the killer rides with a brand that matches Cade’s outer seals like a signature on arson. Quill’s warrant names him Vern of Lowhedge. “Alive if possible,” Quill says. “Dead if the world insists on being itself.”

frames.body: Somebody buried a whip and marked the dirt with hope. Your wrist freemark itches whenever eyes linger too long. You walk on because stopping is how numbers return.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Stubborn Mile
frames.body: Powder grit under your nail smells like unfinished court. Quill says, “Pretty country. Ugly ownership. Pass the damn bottle.” West is weather; east is a bill that grins.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Ring Memory
frames.body: Night insects keep score of every secret step you still take. A joke dies mid-laugh when Cade’s check appears on a sleeve. Collar mud still clings; freemark ink is drying on purpose.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: East Debt
frames.body: Bootleather remembers every mile better than maps ever will. Ash learns freeman posture: chin up, exits counted, no bowing to seals. The vow stays clean enough to cut; dust can have the rest.

frames.title: Dust Wargs
frames.body: Two dust-wargs burst from thorn. Their eyes reflect fire they did not start and do not regret. Quill covers the left; yours is the right throat — if you still remember what cages taught about hesitation.

choices.label: Stand and fight
choices.approach: Use hotbar steel; clocks stay off — take your time.

outcome.text: Dust-Warg Pair falls. The road tastes like iron and relief.

choices.label: Outwit the terrain
choices.approach: Use cover, noise, and Quill’s nasty little tricks.

success.text: The ambush becomes your board. They break first.

fail.text: Clever turns loud. You bleed for the lesson and win anyway.

choices.label: Break past and run
choices.approach: Freedom first; honor later.

outcome.text: Dust and speed. Quill calls it strategic cowardice — fondly.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Dust Mile
frames.body: A hanged scarecrow wears Cade’s check like a joke that landed. Quill mutters law under his breath like a protective curse, then swears for emphasis. Lyra’s quiet ticks quieter than a clock and louder than leftover fear.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Quiet Counsel
frames.body: A child’s doll face-down in soot teaches Vern’s sermon without words. Collar-yard gossip rides cheaper than truth and farther than mercy. Collar mud still clings; freemark ink is drying on purpose.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Road Hygiene
frames.body: Quill hums a tune that never finishes the same way twice. The next mile wears emptiness like a cheap disguise. Darkness checks the list; you give it a name instead.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Old Hunger
frames.body: Road dust writes temporary freemarks on every boot. Quill’s tea tastes like boiled warrants, patience, and whiskey that lost an argument. Every mile files its own ugly receipt.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Freemark Thoughts
frames.body: East wind brings sugar lies and iron honesty in the same breath. Ash rolls a shoulder that still remembers iron’s manners. You keep moving because rust has a longer memory than rest — and quit is not a word you own.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Ledger Weather
frames.body: A warrant nail still bleeds pine sap where paper used to hang. You drink hope and suspicion from one skin; both taste like metal. Another mile turns over, dust-toothed and unpaid.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Steel Practice
frames.body: Wind carries iron smell farther than any honest voice. The first warrant tastes like ash roofs and unfinished graves. Quill packs the silence like powder — dry, useful, dangerous.

frames.title: Vern Cornered
frames.body: Vern sits against a tree with a leg broken by his own horse — comedy the forest did not request. He begs for Cade’s protection and offers a torn ledger page with women’s names. Lyra’s sits three lines down, sold as “song-thrall, unbroken.” Vern smiles like that should buy him sunrise.

choices.label: Bind him for trial
choices.approach: Alive for Quill’s warrant and a clean conscience.

outcome.text: Vern weeps gratitude he does not deserve. Quill stamps ALIVE like it hurts. The ledger page is yours.

choices.label: End him for Lowhedge
choices.approach: Justice for ash roofs; take the page either way.

outcome.text: The forest swallows the sound. Quill files the warrant CLOSED and does not congratulate you.

choices.label: Press for Candlemire routes
choices.approach: Pain or promise — get the eastern road correct.

success.text: Vern sketches fords and bribe posts. “Cade smiles when he sells people,” he spits. “You’ll hate how pretty it is.”

fail.text: Vern faints before finishing. You still have the ledger shred, a headache, and Quill’s unimpressed silence.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Night Watch
frames.body: Crows argue over something dead you hope is not destiny. A road-vermin kid offers directions priced in half-truths. Small dangers flinch; the real ones smile and follow.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Iron Humor
frames.body: The road offers a fork that is really a dare. Quill drawls that quitters make excellent fertilizer and worse freemen. Far off, iron complains one last time and goes quiet.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Collar Echo
frames.body: Overseer laughter carries farther than any hymn on this road. You check the freemark and find it still yours — for now. You walk west, then south, then wherever vengeance keeps maps — stubborn as weather.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Warrant Weather
frames.body: Clouds drag shadows across cane-colored grass like slow warrants. You practice stance until rage becomes usable instead of loud. Continue — soft roads make soft collars again.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Hard Mercy
frames.body: Cage wheels still squeak in the memory of your spine. Road vermin sell water and buy stories about numbered men who would not quit. Ash does not quit; Quill drinks to the fact and almost smiles.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Vermin Bargain
frames.body: Quill counts cartridges the way priests count sins — carefully, then drinks anyway. Ash keeps counsel; Quill keeps a ledger of silences and a flask of worse truths. The cane smell thickens like a dare.

frames.title: Road Wargs Ambush
frames.body: A collar ring half-buried in mud still remembers a neck. Warrant ink under your coat weighs more than the steel above it. The cane smell thickens like a dare. Then road wargs force the issue — a roadside lesson Quill refuses to skip.

choices.label: Stand and fight
choices.approach: Use hotbar steel; clocks stay off — take your time.

outcome.text: Road Wargs falls. The road tastes like iron and relief.

choices.label: Outwit the terrain
choices.approach: Use cover, noise, and Quill’s nasty little tricks.

success.text: The ambush becomes your board. They break first.

fail.text: Clever turns loud. You bleed for the lesson and win anyway.

choices.label: Break past and run
choices.approach: Freedom first; honor later.

outcome.text: Dust and speed. Quill calls it strategic cowardice — fondly.

frames.title: Name on Paper
frames.body: You say Lyra aloud until the vowels stop shaking and start sounding like a plan. Quill packs Vern’s routes — or what remains of them — and points toward a freeman town that stamps men as unpaid labor instead of numbered iron. Dawn smells like pine, powder, and unfinished debts.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Quill's Aside
frames.body: Marshal Halbrecht Quill lights a match on a warrant nail and looks almost merciful. The first warrant tastes like ash roofs and unfinished graves. Collar mud still clings; freemark ink is drying on purpose.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Cane Wind
frames.body: Distant cane fields clap like soft applause for bad systems. Rations taste like postponement, pepper, and old promises. Freedom keeps charging rent — pay it in miles.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Binder Math
frames.body: Marshal Halbrecht Quill lights a match off a warrant nail, drinks, and looks almost human. Ash refuses to romanticize the road; the road returns the favor. Quill salutes the undone work with whiskey and a brim.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Flask Sermon
frames.body: Chain-Road mist sits on the world like damp wool and worse math. The Wilderland watches and declines to explain itself. Ash sets the pace like a man owed a name and too stubborn to renegotiate.

frames.title: Chain-Road Dawn: Yard Gossip
frames.body: A roadside shrine wears three names and one empty nail. A thrall brand on a fence rail dares you to look away. The road bills in blood, dust, and unfinished names.
